reasoning: With the 360-day average at approximately 0.500, the reference threshold is strictly less than 1. Since fatalities must be integers, the question essentially resolves to whether there will be at least 1 fatality in the 30-day windows prior to each date. Given 6 fatalities over the preceding 360 days, and assuming they occurred in roughly 3-4 separate incidents (such as landmine explosions or border skirmishes), the base rate probability of any given 30-day period seeing >= 1 fatality is around 25-30%. The first period roughly corresponds to this base rate. As the forecast horizon increases for periods 2 and 3, uncertainty grows due to potential shifts in border dynamics, pace of demining, and seasonal changes, so the probabilities are drawn closer to 0.5 as instructed.
